channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Curated geopolitical news headlines from global sources

item.title: 🇮🇱 Israel signals return to war in Gaza as ceasefire talks stall
item.description: Israel has expanded military control over 60% of Gaza while hardening disarmament demands, with analysts warning the Mladenov peace proposal is deliberately geared toward rejection to justify renewed assault. Senior Israeli military officials say the best time to defeat Hamas is now.

item.title: 🇦🇲🇪🇺 Armenia hosts EU summit in pivot away from Russia
item.description: Armenia held its first-ever EU-Armenia summit in Yerevan, signaling a historic shift toward Europe as public sentiment turns against Russia. Moscow has responded with cyberattacks and disinformation campaigns ahead of June elections.

item.title: 🇹🇭🇰🇭 Thailand cancels 25-year oil pact with Cambodia after border clashes
item.description: Thailand scrapped a 2001 agreement for joint offshore energy exploration with Cambodia, forcing Phnom Penh to pursue UN arbitration. The move follows armed conflict between the neighbors last year.

item.title: 🇹🇩⚔️ Boko Haram kills 23 Chadian soldiers in Lake Chad attack
item.description: Boko Haram militants attacked a military base on Lake Chad, killing at least 23 troops and wounding dozens more. President Deby vowed to eradicate the group despite claiming it had no sanctuary in Chad last year.

item.title: 🇺🇦⚖️ Ukraine corruption probe shadows top US negotiator
item.description: Ukraine National Security Council chief Rustem Umerov faces renewed scrutiny over leaked tapes linking him to a major corruption scandal, even as he leads delicate peace negotiations with Washington and Moscow.

item.title: 🇦🇪🇮🇷⚔️ UAE Under Iranian Missile and Drone Attack as US Says Ceasefire Holds
item.description: The United Arab Emirates reported Iranian missile and drone attacks even as Washington maintained a shaky US-Iran ceasefire was intact. US forces exchanged fire while attempting to keep the Strait of Hormuz open for shipping.

item.title: 🇺🇦🇷🇺💀 Russian Attacks Kill at Least 20 Before Rival Ceasefires Take Effect
item.description: Russia and Ukraine proposed competing ceasefire dates around May 9 victory celebrations. Russian drone and missile strikes killed at least 20 people before any truce could begin, with Zelensky condemning Moscow's "cynicism."

item.title: 🇺🇸💰📉 US Trade Deficit Widens as AI Investment Boom Drives Imports Higher
item.description: The US trade deficit widened in March as an artificial intelligence investment surge pulled in imports, offsetting increased exports including crude oil shipments amid the Middle East conflict.

item.title: 🇹🇷🛡️ Turkey Accelerates Delivery of Steel Dome Air Defense System Components
item.description: Turkish defense firm Aselsan will ramp up deliveries of parts for Turkey's integrated multi-layered "Steel Dome" air defense system in 2026, strengthening national air defense capabilities.

item.title: 🇧🇷🇦🇪✈️ Brazil Embraer Eyes New Middle East Defense Deals After UAE Order
item.description: Brazilian aerospace company Embraer expects new defense aircraft deals in the Middle East, including government-to-government procurements, following a landmark C-390 military plane order from the UAE.

item.title: 🇺🇦🇷🇺 Russian Strike Kills Five at Ukraine Gas Production Facilities
item.description: A Russian missile and drone attack on Ukraine's energy infrastructure killed five people overnight at Naftogaz gas production facilities. Zelenskiy denounced Moscow's ceasefire offer as cynical amid continuing strikes.

item.title: 🏛️🇷🇺 Russian Pavilion Reopens at Venice Biennale Despite Controversy
item.description: Russia returned to the Venice Biennale for the first time since its 2022 invasion of Ukraine, opening its pavilion amid bitter controversy and protests from Ukrainian officials and activists.

item.title: ⚔️ Pentagon Chief: Iran Ceasefire Holds Despite Hormuz Clash
item.description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth affirmed the ceasefire with Iran remains in effect, even as U.S. and Iranian forces exchanged fire near the Strait of Hormuz. The U.S. is conducting temporary maritime operations to protect commercial vessels.

item.title: 🇰🇷🇺🇸 South Korea rejects Trump call to join Hormuz mission
item.description: Seoul says UN mandate and global coalition required before joining US-led operation to reopen the Strait of Hormuz after Iranian fire on ships.

item.title: 🇪🇺🇺🇦 EU joins new court to prosecute Putin for war of aggression
item.description: The European Union formally joined the Special Tribunal, the only court with mandate to prosecute Russia for the crime of aggression against Ukraine.

item.title: 🇺🇦⚔️ Russian fiber-optic FPV drones reach Kramatorsk
item.description: Advanced Russian drones with fiber-optic guidance have appeared in Kramatorsk, posing a new threat to one of Ukraine key strongholds in Donetsk Oblast.

item.title: 📉 US Service Sector Cools Sharply as Energy Prices Bite
item.description: U.S. services sector growth slowed for a second month in April, with new orders dropping the most in three years as energy prices from the Iran conflict pushed input costs to their highest since late 2022.

item.title: 🇮🇳 India Infrastructure Giant L&T Reports Profit Drop as Middle East War Disrupts Projects
item.description: Larsen & Toubro posted a 3.1% fall in quarterly profit as the Middle East conflict disrupted the infrastructure giants projects in the region, highlighting economic spillover from the Iran war.

item.title: 💰 Indian Rupee Hits Record Low as US-Iran Strains Mount
item.description: The Indian rupee slid to a record low as a fragile Middle East truce came under strain after the US and Iran launched new attacks to wrestle for control of the Strait of Hormuz, a key artery for global energy supplies.

item.title: 🇹🇼 Pacific Nations Face Fuel Shortages as Iran War Strains Energy Markets
item.description: Countries across Asia are uniting to support nations struggling with high costs due to soaring fuel and energy prices in the wake of the Iran war, with Pacific states particularly exposed to supply disruptions.

item.title: 🛢️ US Energy Exports Surge as Domestic Fuel Prices Draw Scrutiny
item.description: US exporters have helped plug key global energy shortages caused by the Iran war, but sharply rising domestic fuel costs are raising questions about the merits of sending so much overseas while pump prices climb at home.

item.title: 🇨🇳 US Warns of Visa Sanctions on China Over Repatriation Slowdown
item.description: A senior Trump administration official warned that Washington was prepared to increase travel restrictions on China if Beijing does not speed up repatriation of Chinese nationals in the US illegally.

item.title: 🇺🇳 US and Bahrain push UN resolution for Hormuz maritime coalition
item.description: UN Security Council members begin talks on a US-Bahrain backed draft resolution that could authorize a multinational maritime coalition to secure the Strait of Hormuz amid Iran's effective closure of the waterway.

item.title: 🛢️ Iraq offers deep discounts for tankers braving Hormuz
item.description: Iraq has offered to cut crude oil prices by more than 25% for shipping firms willing to risk transit through the Strait of Hormuz, as hundreds of ships remain stranded off Dubai.

item.title: 🇹🇷 Turkish company signs deal to export 100,000 kamikaze drones
item.description: Turkish defense firm Pasifik Teknoloji announced a major agreement to sell tens of thousands of FPV kamikaze drones, highlighting Turkey's growing defense export industry.
